preamble: The loss of an initial glottal stop before a high front vowel is a natural path of weakening in many languages. The glottal closure is simply not needed to separate the vowel from silence or a preceding consonant, resulting in a clean vowel onset.

examples.note: Word-initial glottal stop before /i/ is frequently omitted in rapid speech

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
